articles.title: Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game
articles.description:
The video appears to show a South High player fall after a Andover player shoots a basket. Another South High player then appears to push an Andover player.

The post Worcester school officials address racist comments after girls basketball game appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Worcester school officials condemned racist comments made during a girls basketball game.
The incident involved players from South High and Andover, highlighting ongoing racial tensions.
Local leaders emphasize the need for community dialogue to address racism in sports.

articles.title: Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos
articles.description:
In an interview with federal authorities, the man admitted that he had been sexually interested in minors for several years.

The post Rehoboth man allegedly sexually exploited minors, had ‘hundreds’ of illegal images, videos appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Rehoboth man, 35, allegedly exploited minors and possessed hundreds of illegal images.
Federal authorities reported the man admitted to his sexual interest in minors for years.
Local community concerns rise over child safety following these serious allegations.

articles.title: Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets
articles.description:
Repair crews worked nonstop over the course of 48 hours after thousands of gallons of water poured into the streets of Jamaica Plain.

The post Jackson Square intersection reopens after burst water main flooded streets appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jackson Square intersection in Jamaica Plain reopened after a 48-hour repair from a burst water main.
Thousands of gallons of water flooded the streets, disrupting local traffic and businesses.
Residents can now access the intersection safely, improving local transportation and commerce.

articles.title: Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges
articles.description:
Derrick Todd, 52, was indicted on three counts of rape and 17 counts of indecent assault and battery after hundreds of allegations.

The post Former Brigham and Women’s doctor facing new rape charges appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Derrick Todd, 52, indicted on three counts of rape and 17 counts of indecent assault.
Charges stem from hundreds of allegations against the former Brigham and Women’s doctor.
Local residents may feel increased concern over safety in healthcare settings.

articles.title: Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life
articles.description:
John Paul, AAA Northeast's Car Doctor, hears from a reader who is converting a van into a camper.

The post Getting a vehicle ready for Van Life appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: John Paul advises on van conversions for Boston residents interested in van life.
AAA Northeast provides tips for safe vehicle modifications and camping readiness.
Local enthusiasts can enhance travel experiences by preparing vehicles for outdoor adventures.

articles.title: Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify
articles.description:
The former choral director was arrested in August 2025 after a monthslong investigation by Brockton police and State Police.

The post Charges dropped against former Brockton choral director after alleged victim declines to testify appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Charges against former Brockton choral director were dropped on March 19, 2026.
The director was arrested in August 2025 after a lengthy investigation.
Brockton residents may feel relief as the case concludes without testimony.

articles.title: 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown
articles.description:
Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old-boy were arrested for allegedly pointing a gun at an elderly woman before stealing her purse, police said.

The post 18-year-old and 14-year-old charged in daytime armed robbery in Charlestown appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Davonte Jordan, 18, and a 14-year-old charged with armed robbery in Charlestown.
Suspects allegedly threatened an elderly woman with a gun before stealing her purse.
Incident raises concerns about safety for Boston's elderly residents in public spaces.
